description_long: This is a vintage Sony radio receiver with a rectangular metal chassis and a brushed silver finish. The front panel features a large central tuning knob, several smaller control knobs, push buttons, and a horizontal frequency display with indicator lights. The top and sides have ventilation grilles for heat dissipation, while the rear panel is equipped with multiple RCA input/output ports, speaker terminals, and a power connector. The object is robustly built, with visible screws and a solid, industrial design typical of classic audio equipment. The front face is adorned with the Sony logo and model information, and there are colored labels and stickers on both the front and rear panels. The overall appearance suggests a durable, high-quality electronic device designed for home or professional audio use.
description: A vintage Sony radio receiver with a brushed silver metal body, prominent tuning knob, and multiple control features on the front panel. It has ventilation grilles, input/output ports, and speaker terminals on the rear.
description_view_0: The front view shows the Sony logo, a large tuning knob, several control knobs, push buttons, and a horizontal frequency display.
description_view_1: The left side view displays the solid metal side panel with ventilation grilles and no visible controls or ports.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals multiple RCA input/output ports, speaker terminals, a power connector, and various labels and stickers.
description_view_3: The right side view shows another solid metal panel with ventilation grilles, similar to the left side, and a power connector near the bottom.
